name: WeClone
description: 🚀 One-stop solution for creating your AI twin from chat history 💡 Fine-tune LLMs with your chat logs to capture your unique style, then bind to a chatbot to bring your digital self to life.  从聊天记录创造数字分身的一站式解决方案
readme_summary: WeClone is a Python-based solution for creating a personalized AI digital avatar from chat history. It allows users to fine-tune Large Language Models (LLMs) with their chat logs, capturing their unique communication style, and then deploy this model as a chatbot, effectively bringing their digital self to life.
key_features: End-to-end solution for creating digital avatars
LLM fine-tuning with chat history (including image data)
Integration with Telegram and other platforms (WhatsApp, Discord, Slack, WeChat)
primary_use_case: To create a personalized AI digital avatar that mimics a user's communication style by fine-tuning an LLM on their chat history.
cover_image_prompt: A digital sculptor meticulously chiseling a human-like avatar from a block of glowing data streams, representing chat logs. The avatar is beginning to take on a recognizable, personalized form. In the background, subtle UI elements show data flowing from various chat icons (like Telegram) into the sculpting process. The sculptor's tools are stylized as code snippets and data visualizations. The overall mood is one of creation and personalization, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The scene should be rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing the transformation of raw data into a unique digital identity.

name: oxwm
readme_summary: OXWM is a dynamic window manager written in Zig, offering an enhanced alternative to dwm. It provides a flexible Lua API for configuration with hot-reloading, aiming to reduce user friction through sensible defaults, LSP integration, and instant configuration updates without restarting the X session.
primary_use_case: A highly configurable and user-friendly dynamic window manager for Linux/Unix-like systems, written in Zig.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled artisan, representing OXWM, is effortlessly sculpting a complex, dynamic cityscape out of luminous digital clay. Instead of traditional tools, the artisan uses a flowing stream of Lua code that materializes into elegant building structures and interconnected pathways. Small, glowing windows representing different applications float seamlessly within the cityscape, adjusting their positions and sizes with fluid grace. The background is a soft, abstract representation of an X session, with subtle lines of code and data streams hinting at the underlying technology. The overall mood is one of creative control and seamless integration, rendered in a clean, modern 3D isometric illustration style with a vibrant, yet harmonious color palette.

name: Fun-ASR
description: Fun-ASR is an end-to-end speech recognition large model launched by Tongyi Lab.
readme_summary: Fun-ASR is an advanced, end-to-end speech recognition large model developed by Tongyi Lab. It is trained on massive datasets, offering robust contextual understanding and industry-specific adaptability. The model supports low-latency real-time transcription across 31 languages and excels in specialized domains by accurately handling jargon and minimizing errors like hallucinations.
key_features: Trained on tens of millions of hours of real speech data
Accurate recognition of professional terminology and industry expressions
Supports 7 Chinese dialects and 26 regional accents
Covers multiple regional accents for English and Japanese
Lyric recognition and rap speech recognition
primary_use_case: Accurate and efficient speech-to-text transcription, especially in specialized domains and multilingual environments.
cover_image_prompt: A wise, ancient librarian meticulously transcribing spoken words from a swirling cloud of sound into perfectly formed text on a glowing scroll. The librarian uses a magnifying glass that reveals intricate details within the sound waves, separating different voices and accents. The scroll displays clear, legible text in multiple languages, with specific industry terms highlighted. Subtle digital particles representing data flow around the scene, and the repository name 'Fun-ASR' is subtly etched on the librarian's desk. The style is a 3D isometric illustration with a warm, inviting color palette and soft lighting, emphasizing clarity and precision.

name: Nano-PDF
description: Edit PDF files with Nano Banana
readme_summary: Nano-PDF is a Python-based command-line tool that allows users to edit PDF documents using natural language prompts. It leverages Google's Gemini 3 Pro Image model to understand and execute edits, such as updating text or generating new content, while preserving the original document's structure and text layer through OCR.
primary_use_case: Editing PDF documents, particularly presentations or reports, by describing desired changes in natural language, making PDF modification more accessible and efficient.
cover_image_prompt: A whimsical, magical librarian is using a glowing quill to rewrite text on an open PDF document that is floating in mid-air. The PDF document is transforming from an older, static version into a dynamic, vibrant one, with new elements like charts and images appearing. Subtle digital particles and faint code snippets surround the librarian, hinting at the AI power. The background is a cozy, modern study with soft lighting. The overall style should be a 3D isometric illustration, emphasizing clarity and the transformative power of natural language editing on digital documents. The repository name 'Nano-PDF' is subtly etched on the librarian's desk.

name: pgschema
description: Terraform-style, declarative schema migration CLI for Postgres. Agent friendly.
readme_summary: pgschema is a Go-based CLI tool that enables Terraform-style declarative schema migrations for PostgreSQL. It allows users to define their desired database schema state and automatically generates the necessary SQL migration DDL, simplifying the migration process and supporting advanced PostgreSQL features.
key_features: Automatic DDL generation from desired state
Support for advanced PostgreSQL-specific objects (RLS, partitioning, triggers, etc.)
primary_use_case: Automating and simplifying PostgreSQL schema migrations using a declarative, Terraform-like approach, especially for complex schemas with advanced PostgreSQL features.
cover_image_prompt: A master architect standing before a detailed blueprint of a futuristic PostgreSQL database. Instead of drawing lines by hand, the architect uses a magical tablet that instantly generates complex structures like partitioned tables and RLS policies as glowing, interconnected blocks. Small digital particles representing data flow from the tablet to the blueprint, showing the automated generation of the schema. The scene is clean and modern, with subtle UI elements of the pgschema CLI visible on a nearby screen. The overall mood is one of precision and effortless creation. 3D isometric illustration style with a vibrant, tech-focused color palette.
